dimensions.label: Title structure
dimensions.leader_pattern: Brand + Product Type + Pack Size + Duration Supply + Use Case keywords. Terro packs title with SKU code + multiple use-case locations.
dimensions.recommendation: Add specific use-case keywords (kitchen, countertop, home), include lure duration in title, add SKU code for branded search.

dimensions.label: Main keyword focus
dimensions.leader_pattern: Terro: 'fruit fly trap indoor'; Aunt Fannie's: 'fruit fly trap kitchen'; Super Ninja: 'fruit fly trap indoor'
dimensions.recommendation: Target both 'fruit fly trap' and 'fruit fly trap indoor kitchen' for maximum coverage.

dimensions.label: Key claims
dimensions.leader_pattern: All leaders claim fast-acting + long-lasting. Terro differentiates on design (apple shape). Aunt Fannie's on safety (plant-based). Super Ninja on speed (24h results).
dimensions.recommendation: Need a unique speed or efficacy proof point. Quantifiable claims (24h, 45 days) outperform vague 'effective' messaging.

dimensions.label: Hero message
dimensions.leader_pattern: Leaders lead with a single memorable differentiator: Terro = apple design + 180d supply, Aunt Fannie's = plant-based safety, Super Ninja = 24h results.
dimensions.recommendation: Pick ONE hero differentiator and make it the first thing buyers see. Avoid generic 'effective and safe' messaging.

dimensions.label: Image strategy
dimensions.leader_pattern: Top sellers use 8-9 image slots. All have: hero, lifestyle, infographic, comparison. Terro and HOT SHOT use all 9 slots (MAIN + PT01-PT08).
dimensions.recommendation: Fill all 9 image slots. Must-haves: comparison image (vs. DIY/alternatives), infographic (how mechanism works), lifestyle (kitchen counter).

dimensions.label: Content assets
dimensions.leader_pattern: All 5 have A+ Content. Terro and Super Ninja have listing video. Only Aunt Fannie's and HOT SHOT lack video.
dimensions.recommendation: A+ Content is table stakes. Video significantly boosts conversion — prioritize a 30-60s product demo video.

gaps.gap: HOT SHOT doesn't mention safety/non-toxic at all — major gap given competitor emphasis
Aunt Fannie's only offers 30 days per bottle vs. Terro's 45 days — weak longevity claim
Aunt Fannie's uses only 7 slots (missing PT01) vs. 9 for Terro/HOT SHOT
Aunt Fannie's and HOT SHOT have no listing video
Only Super Ninja claims specific timeframe (24h). Others say 'few days' — weaker proof point
Only Super Ninja mentions eco/recyclable — potential differentiator in this market
